title: yosys synth_ice40 infers incorrect number of BRAMS for some memory sizes
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.30+38 (git sha1 2310a0ea9, clang 10.0.0-4ubuntu1 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

Compile this with: `yosys -p "synth_ice40 -top top -json top.json" top.v`

```
module top (
    input                               clk,
    input wire [16:0]                   addr1,
    input wire [16:0]                   addr2,
    input wire [7:0]                    data1,
    output reg [7:0]                    data2
    );

    // Fail : NUM_BRAMS(Actual BRAMS) = 23(24), 27(28), 31(32)
    //      : All other values of NUM_BRAMS between 1 => 32 pass/match.
    localparam NUM_BRAMS = 27;

    reg [7:0] mem1 [0:(512*NUM_BRAMS)-1];

    always @(posedge clk) begin
        mem1[addr1] <= data1;
        data2 <= mem1[addr2];
    end

endmodule
```

Try setting NUM_BRAMS to 23, 27, or 31 and see an extra mystery BRAM is consumed.
Others I am collaborating with have reported identical failures on versions:

Yosys 0.49+17 (git sha1 f2d96cf61, g++ 12.2.0-14 -fPIC -O3)  [WSL2 Debian Bookworm]
Yosys 0.23 (git sha1 7ce5011c24b)  [on Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm) Kernel: Linux 6.6.74+rpt-rpi-v8]

This problem does NOT appear to be an issue on yosys version:

Yosys 0.9 (git sha1 1979e0b)  [raspberry pi release: Debian GNU/Linux 11 (bullseye)]


### Expected Behavior

Expecting the number of BRAMs to be correlated to the inferred memory size.

### Actual Behavior

One extra BRAM appears to get allocated for some certain specific sizes (see example code.)
